1.title: The shortest of letters
1.body: Second John is among the shortest books in the Bible—just thirteen verses. Its author calls himself simply “the elder,” understood to be the Apostle John.

1.template: The elder unto the elect {0} and her children

1.question: What do the “many deceivers” refuse to confess (v. 7)?
options: That Jesus Christ is come in the flesh
That the law of Moses is fulfilled
That the temple will be rebuilt

1.template: He that abideth in the {0} of Christ, he hath both the Father and the Son

1.title: Paper and ink
1.body: John says he would rather not write with “paper and ink” but speak face to face—a glimpse of how letters were carried between early Christian congregations.

1.question: Rather than writing everything with paper and ink, what did John hope to do (v. 12)?
options: Come and speak face to face
